title: ds-viz-pie - option matrix (light)
description: Test bench for splunk.pie in light theme. Each panel exercises one option group: labelDisplay (values / valuesAndPercentage / off), donut hole, collapseThreshold / collapseLabel, named colors, sorted vs unsorted slices, single-color emphasis, and the chrome-stripped KPI tile pattern. SPL is makeresults-based so this dashboard runs in any Splunk instance with no setup.

options.query: | makeresults count=5 | streamstats count as i | eval region = case(i=1,"US-East", i=2,"US-West", i=3,"EU-West", i=4,"APAC", i=5,"LATAM"), revenue = case(i=1,420000, i=2,310000, i=3,265000, i=4,180000, i=5,95000) | sort - revenue | table region revenue
| makeresults count=3 | streamstats count as i | eval browser = case(i=1,"Chrome", i=2,"Safari", i=3,"Edge"), share = case(i=1,62, i=2,24, i=3,14) | sort - share | table browser share

| makeresults count=12 | streamstats count as i | eval country = case(i=1,"United States", i=2,"United Kingdom", i=3,"Germany", i=4,"France", i=5,"Japan", i=6,"Canada", i=7,"Australia", i=8,"Brazil", i=9,"Spain", i=10,"Italy", i=11,"Norway", i=12,"Denmark"), revenue = case(i=1,1200000, i=2,420000, i=3,330000, i=4,210000, i=5,180000, i=6,90000, i=7,42000, i=8,18000, i=9,12000, i=10,9000, i=11,5000, i=12,3000) | sort - revenue | table country revenue

| makeresults count=4 | streamstats count as i | eval status = case(i=1,"healthy", i=2,"degraded", i=3,"unhealthy", i=4,"unknown"), hosts = case(i=1,840, i=2,46, i=3,12, i=4,6) | sort - hosts | table status hosts
| makeresults count=2 | streamstats count as i | eval action = case(i=1,"allowed", i=2,"blocked"), count = case(i=1,9420, i=2,580) | sort - count | table action count

options.markdown:
# `splunk.pie` - option matrix (light)

A single-purpose chart for **part-of-whole** with a small number of slices. The 10.4 reference has only nine documented options: `label`, `labelField`, `labelDisplay`, `value`, `valueField`, `seriesColors`, `seriesColorsByField`, `collapseLabel`, `collapseThreshold`, `showDonutHole`, plus `backgroundColor` and `resultLimit`. Pie has **no axis, no legend position, no annotations, no overlays, no stacking** - if you reach for one of those, you are in the wrong viz family.

viz_basic.title: 1. Minimal - default labelDisplay (values)
viz_basic.description: Default labelDisplay shows the value next to each slice label. Five regions, sorted descending in SPL. Slice colours come from the default Splunk palette.

viz_label_pct.title: 2. labelDisplay - valuesAndPercentage
viz_label_pct.description: labelDisplay=valuesAndPercentage adds the %-of-total. Use when both raw and proportional values matter. The most informative default for executive dashboards.

viz_label_off.title: 3. labelDisplay - off (legend-only)
viz_label_off.description: labelDisplay=off hides labels on the slices themselves. Reader has to map colour-to-meaning via the legend (always rendered automatically). Useful for tightly-cropped panels where labels would clip.

viz_donut.title: 4. Donut - showDonutHole + values+pct
viz_donut.description: showDonutHole=true cuts a centre hole. Easier to read because slice arcs (not slice areas) carry the message. Default shape for KPI-style breakdown panels.

viz_named_colors.title: 5. seriesColorsByField - traffic-light semantics
viz_named_colors.description: seriesColorsByField pins colour to slice label. Survives SPL field reorder. Use for any chart where colour means something (healthy=green, degraded=amber, unhealthy=red).

viz_collapse.title: 6. collapseThreshold + collapseLabel
viz_collapse.description: Long-tail dataset (12 countries). collapseThreshold=0.02 buckets every slice <2% into a single 'Other' slice (collapseLabel). Cuts visual noise without losing the total. Default threshold is 0.01 (1%).

viz_donut_collapse.title: 7. Donut + collapseThreshold (executive look)
viz_donut_collapse.description: Combination that works for almost any executive overview: donut shape, long tail collapsed at 5%, percentage labels. Reduces 12 slices to ~5 readable arcs.

viz_two_slice.title: 8. Two slices (donut) - allow / block
viz_two_slice.description: Two-slice donut is the simplest possible breakdown. Use only when there are exactly two categories and the headline number is the percentage of one of them. seriesColorsByField for clear allow/block colours.

viz_emphasis.title: 9. Single-color emphasis
viz_emphasis.description: All slices share a single hue, varying lightness. Removes the rainbow effect when the takeaway is the SHAPE (one dominant slice + tail), not category identity.

viz_donut_chrome.title: 10. Chrome-stripped donut - KPI tile
viz_donut_chrome.description: labelDisplay=off + transparent backgroundColor turns the donut into a pure shape for embedding inside a KPI card. Pair with a singlevalue overlay sitting on top of the hole for the classic dashboard pattern.

viz_threshold_aggressive.title: 11. collapseThreshold=0.10 - hard collapse
viz_threshold_aggressive.description: Threshold of 10%. Anything under 10% goes into Other. Only the top three countries survive as their own slices. Use when the top-N is the message.

viz_basic_pie_no_pct.title: 12. Five slices, default values only
viz_basic_pie_no_pct.description: Same data as panel 1 but without percentages. Use when the underlying counts are more meaningful than the proportions (revenue numbers vs. share-of-revenue).
